thread.title: Plano City Council chambers to reopen following renovations
thread.title_full: Plano City Council chambers to reopen following renovations

author: Michael Crouchley
title: Plano City Council chambers to reopen following renovations
text:
[Plano’s](https://www.plano.gov/)Sen. Florence Shapiro Council Chamber is set to reopen Dec. 2 following eight months of construction.
The gist
The chambers at 1520 K Ave., in the Plano Municipal Center, will begin hosting City Council and planning and zoning commission meetings again Dec. 2.
The chambers
[closed for renovations April 1](https://communityimpact.com/dallas-fort-worth/plano/government/2024/03/12/davis-library-to-host-plano-city-meetings-starting-april-1/), and meetings have since been held at
[Davis Library](https://www.plano.gov/989/Davis-Library).
The $6 million project was funded through the city’s
[2021 bond](https://www.plano.gov/1946/2021-Bond-Referendum)and Public Education Government Funds. The renovations focused on the following areas:
- Removing barriers to improve accessibility in the council chambers
- Updating the audio, video and acoustical components
- Upgrading security
More information on the renovations can be found on
[the city's website.](https://www.plano.gov/2054/Council-Chambers-Renovation-Project)
